task_completion.description: Locate and open the Jitsi Meet configuration file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal or text editor shows the agent accessing the file at '/home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/config.js'.

task_completion.description: Edit the configuration file to enable lobby mode by default.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows modifications to the config file, such as adding 'lobby: { autoEnable: true }' or similar settings intended to enable the lobby globally.

task_completion.description: Restart the Jitsi Meet service to apply the changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal shows a command like 'docker restart jitsi-web-1', 'docker-compose restart', or 'docker compose restart' being executed.

task_completion.description: Access the local Jitsi Meet web interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open displaying the local Jitsi Meet homepage.
The web browser shows the agent entering 'SecureTest' as the meeting name and joining it, with 'SecureTest' visible in the URL or meeting title.

task_completion.description: Confirm that Lobby Mode is active immediately upon joining.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the 'SecureTest' meeting, a notification or the security settings menu indicates that Lobby Mode is already enabled automatically upon joining.

integrity.description: The agent must not manually enable Lobby Mode through the GUI to fake the automatic configuration.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is not seen clicking the toggle to turn on Lobby Mode in the meeting's security options.

integrity.description: The agent tested the meeting creation in a real web browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: A graphical web browser is used to access the Jitsi instance and join the 'SecureTest' meeting, rather than relying solely on terminal outputs or curl commands.

integrity.description: The agent actually restarted the service to apply changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal history shows docker restart commands for the Jitsi service after the config file was edited.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Jitsi Meet configuration file is located at /home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/config.js. The agent may attempt to add 'lobby: { autoEnable: true }' to this file, but this is NOT a valid Jitsi Meet configuration option in version stable-9753. Consequently, Lobby Mode will likely NOT be active automatically when the agent tests the 'SecureTest' meeting. Score the final verification step strictly based on whether Lobby Mode is visually confirmed to be active immediately upon joining, without manual toggling.
